svg.font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, Segoe UI, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif
svg.text: joshuamandel.com/fhir-ig-editor · everything client-side after first load · solid = live today · dashed = in flight / planned (F-phases)
CI (GitHub Actions) → GitHub Pages — build-time, hermetic
E2E gate drives the LIVE url post-deploy
baked cycle · GitHub repo · local dir
ValueSet expansion tab (tier-1, live per keystroke)
design assets via <base>, images via blob:
same site.db under all of them
Web Worker — typed protocol: compile · snapshot · buildSiteDb · expand · renderPage · fileOps
fetch bundle on first engine need
init({rows, fragments, assets, template?}) → listPages() → renderPage(slug) → html
fragment(ref, kind) → key = hash(resource, kind, opts, renderer-ver) → cache hit? : wasm render_fragment
shared by ALL adapters: custom TS chrome may embed publisher-grade snapshot/dict/expansion tables
no eager menu (Publisher wastes 68%) · no template scanning (rapido's regex) · staleness is structural
wasm engine — ONE instance, state stays resident between calls (crates: compiler · snapshot_gen · site_db · wasm_api)
pure fn of IG content: local CS +
tables C2 + sd_render C1 (the XL critical path) →
trait over all package reads (corpus 955/955 gated over it) · browser mounts bundles in-memory
Layer B (opt-in, off in editor): CoreVersionPinner + R4 artifact projection
tier-1 expansion cache key discipline shared with committed CI cache (consistency gate)
node = {fsh file, resource, snapshot(url), expansion(vs), row group, fragment(ref,kind), md page}
recompute only when input hash changed · S1/S2 tank-global by design (sub-second anyway)
edit flow: FSH change → resource hash changes → dependent snapshot/fragment/row nodes dirty →
Ledger 2 replays page read-sets → only dirty pages re-render
— external CS content is GB-scale, never local —
result → OPFS cache with expansion.parameter pin
first load: app + wasm + manifests (bundles fetched lazily)
edit FSH → worker compile (~90 ms) → changed resource hashes dirty their snapshot/row/fragment nodes (Ledger 1) →
adapter re-renders ONLY the visible / read-set-dirty page (Ledger 2) → page includes hit FragmentApi → cache miss? wasm renders that ONE fragment (ms) →
iframe updates. US Core's 70 profiles never render eagerly — only pages you visit materialize, and only fragments those pages include are ever generated.
Contrast: the Java Publisher eagerly generates the complete fragment menu (68% never included by the template) on every build.
